skill.name: competitive-ads-extractor
skill.description: Guides competitive advertising research by collecting public ad examples, summarizing messaging themes, and identifying creative patterns.
skill.summary: Analyze public competitor ads to extract messaging, creative, and positioning insights.
skill.author: DYAI2025

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer reports for Ruby or shell backtick execution are false positives caused by Markdown code fences in SKILL.md. Weak cryptographic algorithm reports are also false positives caused by ordinary prose, but the skill does instruct agents to scrape public ad libraries and save screenshots or reports locally, which creates operational compliance and data handling risk.

medium_findings.title: Public Web Scraping and Local Artifact Creation
medium_findings.description: The skill directs agents to scrape public ad libraries and save screenshots, reports, CSV files, or presentations. This appears legitimate for competitive research, but it can create platform terms, copyright, and local data retention risk if used without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is explicitly described in the skill instructions and output formats. It is not malicious by itself, but it relies on network collection and local storage of third-party ad materials.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer matched triple-backtick Markdown fences and example prompts as Ruby or shell backtick execution. SKILL.md is documentation text, and no executable Ruby, shell command, or command interpolation is present at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every cited location is inside Markdown prose or fenced examples. I found no code that would execute the fenced text as a command.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Matches in Prose
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography findings are keyword collisions in ordinary text, including the frontmatter description, the phrase 'Provides Insights', and a warning about stealing designs. No cryptographic function or weak algorithm call is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are natural-language documentation and do not reference crypto APIs, hashes, ciphers, or security-sensitive transformations.

content.value_statement: Competitive ad research is slow when examples are scattered across public ad libraries. This skill helps Claude, Codex, or Claude Code organize public ads into messaging, creative, and positioning insights.
actual_capabilities: Guides extraction of public competitor ads from ad libraries.
Organizes ad examples by theme, audience, format, and message.
Summarizes repeated pain points, value propositions, and calls to action.
Compares multiple competitors to identify shared and differentiated positioning.
Recommends original campaign angles based on observed patterns.
Defines output formats such as screenshots, reports, spreadsheets, and presentations.
limitations: It depends on public ad library access and available platform data.
It cannot prove ad performance unless engagement or spend data is available.
Users must review platform terms, copyright limits, and privacy obligations.
It provides research guidance, not automated media buying or campaign execution.

use_cases.title: Research competitor messaging
use_cases.description: Collect current competitor ads and summarize the problems, benefits, and audience segments they emphasize.

use_cases.title: Plan creative tests
use_cases.description: Compare ad formats and creative patterns to choose original concepts for A/B testing.

use_cases.title: Map category positioning
use_cases.description: Review several competitors to identify common claims, market gaps, and differentiated messaging opportunities.

prompt_templates.title: Basic competitor review
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze public ads from [competitor] and summarize the main messages, pain points, calls to action, and creative formats.

prompt_templates.title: Platform-specific analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Review public [platform] ads from [competitor]. Group them by audience, message theme, format, and likely campaign objective.

prompt_templates.title: Competitive set comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare public ads from [company A], [company B], and [company C]. Identify shared category messages, unique positions, and gaps we can test.

prompt_templates.title: Trend and campaign planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare [competitor] ads across [time period one] and [time period two]. Explain messaging changes and propose five original test angles.

output_examples.input: Analyze public ads from a project management competitor.
output: Overview of active themes, common formats, and repeated calls to action.
Messaging map covering collaboration, time savings, reporting, and executive visibility.
Recommendations for original ad tests that avoid copying competitor creative.

output_examples.input: Compare three competitors in the same software category.
output: Competitor-by-competitor summary of primary audience, pain point, and offer.
Shared category patterns that may indicate customer demand.
Whitespace opportunities where the user can test differentiated claims.

output_examples.input: Review static image ads against video ads for one competitor.
output: Format comparison by message depth, visual proof, product visibility, and CTA clarity.
Creative principles to adapt into original campaigns.
Caveat that true performance requires platform metrics or campaign data.

best_practices: Use only public ad sources and respect each platform's terms.
Turn observed patterns into original concepts instead of copying ads.
Track findings over time so changes in positioning are visible.
anti_patterns: Do not scrape private, gated, or unauthorized advertising data.
Do not present frequency of public ads as proof of performance.
Do not reuse competitor copy, layouts, or protected creative assets directly.

faq.question: What platforms can this skill support?
faq.answer: It is written for public ad libraries such as Facebook Ad Library and LinkedIn ad examples. Access depends on platform availability.

faq.question: Does it guarantee which ads are performing best?
faq.answer: No. It can infer patterns from visible ads, but true performance requires reliable spend, engagement, or conversion data.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it safe to copy competitor ads found with this skill?
faq.answer: No. Use the research for inspiration and create original copy, visuals, and campaign angles.

faq.question: What should I provide before using it?
faq.answer: Provide competitor names, target platforms, date ranges when relevant, and the business questions you want answered.

faq.question: What review is needed before publication?
faq.answer: Publish with a warning about public web access, local file output, platform terms, and intellectual property limits.
